channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Free Online Course on Promoting Youth Employment 2026 | Get Certified by ITCILO
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Youth unemployment remains one of the biggest global development challenges today. Across many countries, young people continue to face barriers in accessing decent work opportunities, transitioning from education into employment, and securing quality jobs that provide sustainable livelihoods. To help address these challenges, the International Training Centre of the International Labour Organization (ITCILO) has launched […]

The post Free Online Course on Promoting Youth Employment 2026 | Get Certified by ITCILO appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Youth unemployment remains one of the biggest global development challenges today. Across many countries, young people continue to face barriers in accessing decent work opportunities, transitioning from education into employment, and securing quality jobs that provide sustainable livelihoods.

To help address these challenges, the International Training Centre of the International Labour Organization (ITCILO) has launched a new free self-paced online course titled “Promoting Youth Employment: Challenges, Policies and Emerging Opportunities.”

This course provides participants with practical knowledge, policy insights, and emerging labour market trends that can help shape inclusive and sustainable youth employment strategies worldwide.

The program is completely free of charge and comes with an official digital certificate of completion upon successfully finishing the course modules and assessment.

About the Promoting Youth Employment Course

The “Promoting Youth Employment: Challenges, Policies and Emerging Opportunities” course is designed to introduce learners to the International Labour Organization’s (ILO) approach to youth employment.

The course combines:

  • Labour market analysis
  • Youth employment policy strategies
  • Practical development approaches
  • Emerging economic opportunities
  • Inclusion and gender equality frameworks
  • Decent work principles

Participants will gain valuable insights into how governments, organizations, and development practitioners can better support young people in securing meaningful and sustainable employment opportunities.

The course also explores how major global trends such as digitalization, climate change, demographic shifts, and economic transformation are shaping the future of work for young people around the world.

Why Youth Employment Matters

Youth employment is central to achieving inclusive economic growth and sustainable development.

Many young people globally face challenges such as:

  • High unemployment rates
  • Informal employment
  • Limited access to quality jobs
  • Skills mismatches
  • Poor transition from school to work
  • Gender inequality in employment
  • Limited economic opportunities

These challenges are becoming even more complex due to:

  • Rapid technological changes
  • Climate-related disruptions
  • Population growth
  • Economic uncertainty
  • Changing labour market demands

This course helps participants understand these realities while identifying practical policy solutions that can create better employment pathways for young people.

What You Will Learn

By the end of the course, participants will be able to:

1. Understand Global Youth Employment Challenges

Learners will explore the major factors affecting youth employment globally, including unemployment trends, labour market inequalities, and barriers to decent work.

2. Learn Key Labour Market Concepts

The course introduces important labour market concepts used by the International Labour Organization (ILO), helping participants understand employment systems more effectively.

3. Identify Effective Youth Employment Policies

Participants will learn practical policy approaches that can support young people in accessing sustainable and decent employment opportunities.

4. Explore Emerging Employment Sectors

The course highlights employment opportunities in fast-growing sectors such as:

  • Digital economy
  • Green economy
  • Care economy
  • Creative economy

These sectors are increasingly shaping the future global labour market.

5. Understand Inclusion and Gender Equality

Special attention is given to:

  • Gender equality
  • Social inclusion
  • Decent work standards
  • Equitable labour market participation

Participants will learn why inclusive employment strategies are essential for sustainable development.

Course Modules

The course is divided into three structured modules designed to progressively build understanding and practical knowledge.

Module 1 – Understanding the Youth Employment Challenge

This module examines the current realities of youth unemployment and labour market transitions worldwide.

Module 2 – Promoting Youth Employment: Policies and Solutions

Participants explore evidence-based policy approaches and practical solutions that support youth employment.

Module 3 – Promoting Youth Employment in Emerging Sectors

This section focuses on future-oriented employment opportunities in sectors driving economic transformation and sustainability.

Each module includes:

  • Conceptual explanations
  • Real-world examples
  • Interactive exercises
  • Knowledge assessments
  • Reflection activities

Course Duration and Learning Format

The course is fully self-guided, allowing participants to study at their own pace from anywhere in the world.

Duration

  • Approximately 4–5 hours total learning time

Learning Format

  • 100% online
  • Self-paced
  • Flexible learning schedule

This makes it ideal for:

  • Working professionals
  • Government officials
  • Researchers
  • Students
  • Development practitioners

Languages Available

The course is accessible in multiple languages, making it available to a broad international audience.

Available languages include:

  • English
  • French
  • Spanish
  • Portuguese

Certification

Participants who successfully complete the modules and pass the short assessment test will receive an official ITCILO digital certificate of completion.

This certificate can help participants:

  • Strengthen their professional profile
  • Demonstrate expertise in youth employment policy
  • Enhance career opportunities
  • Showcase commitment to sustainable development and labour market inclusion

Who Should Take This Course?

This course is particularly valuable for:

  • Government policymakers
  • Employment officers
  • Youth program coordinators
  • NGO professionals
  • Development practitioners
  • Labour market researchers
  • Academics
  • Trainers and educators
  • International organization staff
  • Students interested in labour and development issues

Anyone interested in understanding the future of youth employment and sustainable labour market solutions can benefit from this course.

Benefits of Joining the Course

Participants can gain several important benefits, including:

  • Free access to expert knowledge
  • Flexible self-paced learning
  • Internationally recognized certificate
  • Practical labour market insights
  • Understanding of emerging job sectors
  • Knowledge of youth employment policy frameworks
  • Enhanced professional development
  • Improved understanding of inclusion and gender equality in employment

How to Enroll

Click here for more and to apply

Since the course is self-paced, participants can start learning immediately after registration.

Final Thoughts

As the future of work continues to evolve rapidly, understanding youth employment challenges and opportunities has become increasingly important for governments, organizations, and development professionals worldwide.

The “Promoting Youth Employment: Challenges, Policies and Emerging Opportunities” course offers a valuable opportunity to gain practical skills and insights from the International Labour Organization’s approach to decent work, inclusion, and sustainable labour market development.

With free enrollment, multilingual access, flexible learning, and certification included, this course is an excellent opportunity for professionals and learners who want to contribute to creating better employment opportunities for young people globally.

Click here for more global training opportunities.

The post Free Online Course on Promoting Youth Employment 2026 | Get Certified by ITCILO appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Policy Center for the New South Opens Applications for Program Officer – Partnerships Position in Rabat, Morocco
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The internationally recognized Policy Center for the New South has officially announced a new career opportunity for professionals interested in international partnerships, economic policy, geopolitics, and African development. The organization is currently recruiting a Program Officer – Partnerships to join its Partnerships Department in Rabat, Morocco. This exciting full-time opportunity is ideal for experienced professionals […]

The post Policy Center for the New South Opens Applications for Program Officer – Partnerships Position in Rabat, Morocco appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The internationally recognized Policy Center for the New South has officially announced a new career opportunity for professionals interested in international partnerships, economic policy, geopolitics, and African development. The organization is currently recruiting a Program Officer – Partnerships to join its Partnerships Department in Rabat, Morocco. This exciting full-time opportunity is ideal for experienced professionals looking to contribute to global policy dialogue, institutional cooperation, and strategic international engagement.

The position offers a chance to work within one of Africa’s leading policy-oriented think tanks, collaborating with international experts, institutions, researchers, and policymakers on issues shaping Africa and the wider Atlantic region. According to the organization, the selected candidate will play an important role in developing and managing strategic partnerships while supporting high-level projects, events, and institutional collaborations.

The Policy Center for the New South is a Morocco-based think tank founded in 2011. The institution focuses on generating policy-oriented research and fostering meaningful dialogue on major economic, geopolitical, and development issues affecting Africa and the Global South. The center has built a strong reputation for connecting policymakers, researchers, civil society actors, and international institutions through conferences, leadership programs, and research initiatives.

About the Program Officer – Partnerships Role

The newly announced Program Officer role is positioned within the Partnerships Department of the institution. The selected professional will contribute directly to strengthening the organization’s relationships with international think tanks, experts, universities, development institutions, and strategic partners.

This role is particularly suited for individuals passionate about:

  • African economic development
  • Geopolitical affairs
  • International cooperation
  • Policy research
  • Global partnerships
  • Strategic engagement
  • International conferences and events

The organization specifically mentioned that the successful applicant will support and develop strategic collaborations while helping coordinate international projects and activities linked to the center’s broader mission.

Key Responsibilities

The Program Officer is expected to support a variety of institutional and partnership-related activities. These include:

1. Strategic Partnership Development

The selected candidate will help identify, establish, and maintain relationships with:

  • International think tanks
  • Academic institutions
  • Policy experts
  • Development organizations
  • Global stakeholders and partners

2. Project Coordination

The role involves supporting the implementation and management of projects connected to:

  • Economic policy
  • International relations
  • African development
  • Atlantic cooperation
  • Institutional collaboration

3. International Engagement

The officer will contribute to:

  • International outreach
  • Partnership engagement
  • Stakeholder communication
  • Cross-border institutional cooperation

4. Event and Activity Management

The selected professional will assist in organizing:

  • Conferences
  • Policy dialogues
  • High-level forums
  • Institutional meetings
  • International engagement activities

This aspect of the role is particularly valuable for candidates seeking exposure to international policy and development ecosystems.

Required Qualifications and Skills

According to the announcement, applicants should possess:

  • Between 3 and 7 years of professional experience
  • Strong writing and communication abilities
  • Excellent coordination and organizational skills
  • Interest in African and Atlantic geopolitical and economic issues
  • Fluency in French, English, and Arabic

Multilingual communication is considered a major requirement because the role involves engagement with international institutions and stakeholders across different regions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This opportunity stands out because it combines:

  • International exposure
  • Policy engagement
  • Strategic networking
  • Professional growth
  • African development impact

Professionals selected for the role will work in a highly international environment where they can engage directly with researchers, policymakers, diplomats, and development experts from around the world.

The Policy Center for the New South is also widely recognized for organizing major international conferences and leadership initiatives focused on Africa’s future, economic transformation, and geopolitical cooperation.

For professionals interested in shaping policy conversations around Africa and the Global South, this role presents a significant opportunity to contribute meaningfully to international dialogue and institutional collaboration.

Work Location and Contract Type

  • Location: Rabat, Morocco
  • Contract Type: CDI (Permanent Contract)

Rabat continues to position itself as an emerging hub for policy research, diplomacy, and international cooperation in Africa and the wider Atlantic region.

Application Information

Interested applicants can apply directly through the official Policy Center for the New South careers platform.

You can get more information about this opportunity on Policy Center for the New South Official Website

Deadline

No official application deadline has been publicly specified yet. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the position is filled

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post Policy Center for the New South Opens Applications for Program Officer – Partnerships Position in Rabat, Morocco appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RestorLife Awards 2026: Win Up to $25,000 in Funding and Global Recognition for Environmental Restoration
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
As climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, and ecosystem destruction continue to threaten communities across the globe, organizations and businesses working to restore nature are becoming more important than ever. To recognize and support these efforts, the RestorLife Awards 2026 is officially open for applications, offering NGOs and responsible businesses the opportunity to gain international […]

The post RestorLife Awards 2026: Win Up to $25,000 in Funding and Global Recognition for Environmental Restoration appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
As climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, and ecosystem destruction continue to threaten communities across the globe, organizations and businesses working to restore nature are becoming more important than ever. To recognize and support these efforts, the RestorLife Awards 2026 is officially open for applications, offering NGOs and responsible businesses the opportunity to gain international recognition, access funding, and showcase their environmental impact on a global stage.

Organizations dedicated to ecological restoration, biodiversity protection, sustainable livelihoods, and environmental conservation now have a limited opportunity to apply before the official deadline of May 31, 2026.

The RestorLife Awards celebrate the inspiring work of communities, restoration champions, NGOs, and businesses that are actively safeguarding ecosystems and creating sustainable futures. The initiative aims to amplify local success stories while providing financial support and international exposure to organizations leading environmental transformation worldwide.

What Are the RestorLife Awards?

The RestorLife Awards are global environmental recognition awards designed to honor organizations and businesses making significant contributions to ecological restoration and sustainable development.

The awards are backed by leading environmental and sustainability stakeholders, including Ma Earth and the UN Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD). The initiative recognizes the importance of community-led environmental action and corporate responsibility in tackling global environmental challenges.

The awards focus on supporting initiatives that:

  • Restore degraded ecosystems
  • Protect biodiversity
  • Improve sustainable livelihoods
  • Support local communities
  • Promote environmental sustainability
  • Encourage responsible business practices
  • Strengthen climate resilience
  • Drive nature-positive action

The 2026 edition of the awards aims to spotlight impactful environmental initiatives from around the world while encouraging more organizations to scale their restoration efforts.

RestorLife Awards 2026 Categories

The RestorLife Awards feature two major categories designed to recognize both non-profit organizations and businesses.

1. NGO Award

The NGO Award is targeted at registered non-profit organizations actively involved in environmental restoration and community-based conservation efforts.

Organizations working on ecosystem protection, biodiversity conservation, land restoration, sustainable agriculture, water conservation, and livelihood improvement are encouraged to apply.

Benefits for NGO Award Finalists and Winners

Selected organizations can receive several major benefits, including:

  1. Global Recognition
    • Organizations could become one of the 6–10 global finalists representing their region.
  2. Crowdfunding Opportunity
    • Finalists will participate in a three-week crowdfunding campaign.
  3. Match Funding Support
    • NGOs may unlock up to US$15,000 in match funding supported by Ma Earth.
  4. International Exposure
    • Winners will be invited to attend UNCCD COP17 2026 in Mongolia.
  5. Additional Grand Prize
    • The winning NGO will receive an exclusive US$10,000 G20 GLI Grand Prize.

2. Responsible Business Leadership Award

The Responsible Business Leadership Award recognizes businesses demonstrating outstanding environmental responsibility and sustainability leadership.

Large businesses actively reducing their environmental footprint and implementing impactful nature-positive initiatives are encouraged to apply.

Benefits for Responsible Businesses

Selected businesses can receive:

  1. Prestigious International Recognition
    • Winners receive honorary recognition by Restor and the G20 GLI Leadership Award.
  2. Global Visibility
    • A professionally developed global case study highlighting the organization’s environmental impact and sustainability initiatives.
  3. International Event Invitation
    • Winners will attend UNCCD COP17 2026 in Mongolia.

Why the RestorLife Awards Matter

Environmental restoration is no longer optional. Around the world, communities are already facing the consequences of climate change, deforestation, biodiversity loss, drought, and ecosystem collapse.

The RestorLife Awards recognize that many impactful environmental solutions are already being led at the grassroots level by local organizations, Indigenous communities, environmental leaders, and responsible companies.

By supporting these initiatives, the awards aim to:

  • Encourage scalable environmental solutions
  • Promote sustainable economic development
  • Inspire global environmental action
  • Increase visibility for local restoration initiatives
  • Connect organizations with international networks
  • Support biodiversity and climate resilience goals

This initiative demonstrates the growing global recognition that restoring nature and protecting livelihoods must happen together.

Who Should Apply?

The RestorLife Awards are ideal for organizations and businesses involved in:

  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Forest restoration
  • Landscape restoration
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Water conservation
  • Climate resilience projects
  • Community-led conservation
  • Sustainable livelihood programs
  • Regenerative environmental initiatives
  • Corporate sustainability programs
  • Nature-positive business practices

Applicants with measurable environmental and social impact will have a stronger chance of standing out during the evaluation process.

Evaluation Process

Applications will be reviewed by a panel of experts specializing in:

  • Ecology
  • Sustainability
  • Community development
  • Environmental restoration
  • Biodiversity conservation

The evaluation process is designed to ensure fairness, credibility, and a strong focus on real-world environmental impact.

Judges will likely assess:

  • Innovation
  • Community impact
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Long-term restoration outcomes
  • Scalability
  • Leadership and collaboration

Key Benefits of Applying

Organizations and businesses applying for the RestorLife Awards 2026 can benefit from:

  • International visibility
  • Potential funding opportunities
  • Global networking opportunities
  • Recognition from respected environmental institutions
  • Increased credibility for future partnerships and grants
  • Exposure at major global environmental events
  • Media and storytelling opportunities

For many organizations, this could become a major stepping stone toward scaling environmental impact globally.

Application Deadline

Applications for the RestorLife Awards 2026 close on May 31, 2026.

Interested organizations and businesses are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to avoid missing this global opportunity.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Applicants are advised to prepare detailed information about their environmental initiatives, community impact, sustainability goals, and measurable restoration outcomes before applying.

Final Thoughts

The RestorLife Awards 2026 represent more than just an award program. They are part of a growing global movement recognizing the organizations, communities, and businesses actively restoring ecosystems and creating sustainable futures.

At a time when environmental action is urgently needed, initiatives like these provide critical funding, recognition, and momentum for organizations driving positive change on the ground.

For NGOs and businesses working tirelessly to protect nature, restore biodiversity, and strengthen livelihoods, this is an opportunity to gain global visibility while accessing meaningful support for future impact.

With the application deadline approaching quickly, eligible organizations should act immediately and submit their applications before May 31, 2026.

Click here for more global Awards.

The post RestorLife Awards 2026: Win Up to $25,000 in Funding and Global Recognition for Environmental Restoration appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Geographic Considerations in Evaluating Women’s Employment Opportunities 2026 – World Bank E-Learning Course on Gender, Geography, and Employment Access
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Geographic Considerations in Evaluating Women’s Employment Opportunities 2026 course is an innovative e-learning program developed to deepen understanding of how geography and spatial environments influence women’s access to employment and economic opportunities. This course introduces learners to the Geospatial Women’s Employment Analytical Framework (GeoWEAF), a cutting-edge tool developed by the World Bank Group. It […]

The post Geographic Considerations in Evaluating Women’s Employment Opportunities 2026 – World Bank E-Learning Course on Gender, Geography, and Employment Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Geographic Considerations in Evaluating Women’s Employment Opportunities 2026 course is an innovative e-learning program developed to deepen understanding of how geography and spatial environments influence women’s access to employment and economic opportunities.

This course introduces learners to the Geospatial Women’s Employment Analytical Framework (GeoWEAF), a cutting-edge tool developed by the World Bank Group. It explores how environmental, social, and infrastructural factors either enable or restrict women’s participation in the labor market.

Through interactive videos, readings, quizzes, and discussions, participants gain practical insights into the relationship between gender equality and geographic conditions shaping employment opportunities.

This training is part of a broader effort to promote inclusive development, gender equality, and evidence-based policymaking in employment systems globally.

About the Course

This e-learning program is designed to help participants understand how location and environment influence women’s ability to access jobs, education, transport, healthcare, and financial services.

It also introduces analytical tools and datasets used in modern gender and spatial analysis.

The course is structured to be flexible, accessible, and practical, making it suitable for professionals and students interested in gender, development, and urban planning.

Key characteristics of the course include:

  • Fully online e-learning format
  • Duration of approximately 2 hours
  • Multiple video-based lessons
  • Text materials and downloadable resources
  • Interactive quizzes and assessments
  • Online discussion forums
  • Certification upon successful completion

Course Objectives

By the end of this course, participants will be able to:

  1. Understand the relationship between geography and gender in employment access
  2. Explore how environmental conditions influence women’s economic opportunities
  3. Learn the GeoWEAF analytical framework developed for gender and spatial analysis
  4. Identify and use open and proprietary data sources for gender-based spatial evaluation
  5. Understand the Gender Enabling Environments Spatial Tool (GEEST) and its applications in policymaking

Course Structure and Modules

The course is divided into six structured learning sections:

1. Geography and Gender Nexus

This module introduces the connection between geography and women’s employment opportunities.

Topics include:

  • Introduction to GeoWEAF framework
  • Geographic barriers affecting women’s employment
  • Career access challenges based on location
  • Pre-course assessment and introductory materials

2. Contextual Dimension

This module explores institutional and structural factors influencing women’s employment.

Key areas include:

  • Workplace discrimination
  • Regulatory frameworks affecting gender equality
  • Financial inclusion and access to economic resources

3. Accessibility Dimension

This module focuses on access to essential services that support employment.

Topics include:

  • Women’s mobility and travel patterns
  • Access to public transportation systems
  • Access to education and training institutions
  • Access to healthcare and financial services

4. Place-Characterization Dimension

This module examines environmental and social conditions shaping women’s opportunities.

Key elements include:

  • Safety and security conditions
  • Conflict and fragility environments
  • Education levels and digital inclusion
  • Environmental hazards and water/sanitation access

5. Spatial Data and Analysis

This module introduces data-driven approaches to gender and geography.

Topics include:

  • Spatial data collection and interpretation
  • Data sources for accessibility analysis
  • Contextual and environmental data evaluation
  • Application of geospatial tools in policymaking

6. Closing Module

The final section includes:

  • Final assessment and evaluation
  • Post-course assessment
  • Feedback submission
  • Certificate issuance
  • Acknowledgments and course completion

What’s Included in the Course

Participants will benefit from a structured learning experience including:

  • Video lectures and expert presentations
  • Text-based learning materials
  • Interactive quizzes and assessments
  • Discussion forums for peer learning
  • Official certification upon completion

Learning Outcomes and Benefits

This course provides strong professional and academic value, especially for individuals working in gender, development, and policy-related fields.

Key benefits include:

  • Strong understanding of gender and geography intersection in employment
  • Practical knowledge of spatial and gender analytical tools
  • Enhanced skills in data interpretation and policy analysis
  • Improved ability to design gender-inclusive development strategies
  • Professional certification from a recognized global institution
  • Exposure to World Bank research methodologies and frameworks

Target Audience

This course is designed for:

  • Urban planners and city development professionals
  • Gender specialists and equality advocates
  • Policymakers and government officials
  • Researchers and academics
  • Students in social sciences, development, and geography
  • Professionals working in social development and employment policy

It is also suitable for anyone interested in understanding how geography affects women’s economic empowerment.

Duration and Format

  • Total duration: 2 hours
  • Format: Online e-learning
  • Content: Videos, texts, quizzes, forums
  • Certification: Available upon completion

Enrollment Information

Enrollment is currently open.

Course access begins on June 16, 2026 for registered participants.

Participants are encouraged to enroll early to secure access and prepare for the learning start date.

Conclusion

The Geographic Considerations in Evaluating Women’s Employment Opportunities 2026 course offers a unique opportunity to explore the intersection of gender, geography, and employment through a scientific and data-driven lens.

By combining theory, practical tools, and global frameworks like GeoWEAF, this course empowers learners to better understand and address the spatial barriers affecting women’s economic participation worldwide.

It is a valuable learning resource for anyone committed to advancing gender equality, inclusive development, and evidence-based policymaking.

 

Apply here 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Training Program opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post Geographic Considerations in Evaluating Women’s Employment Opportunities 2026 – World Bank E-Learning Course on Gender, Geography, and Employment Access appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GAYO Botswana Volunteer and Internship Opportunities 2026 – Call for Applications in Environmental Sustainability, Climate Action, and Youth Leadership Development
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Green Africa Youth Organization Botswana (GAYO Botswana) has officially opened applications for its 2026 Volunteer and Internship Programme. This opportunity is designed for passionate young people who are eager to contribute to environmental sustainability, climate action, zero waste initiatives, and youth empowerment across Botswana and beyond. GAYO Botswana is a youth-led environmental organization that […]

The post GAYO Botswana Volunteer and Internship Opportunities 2026 – Call for Applications in Environmental Sustainability, Climate Action, and Youth Leadership Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Green Africa Youth Organization Botswana (GAYO Botswana) has officially opened applications for its 2026 Volunteer and Internship Programme. This opportunity is designed for passionate young people who are eager to contribute to environmental sustainability, climate action, zero waste initiatives, and youth empowerment across Botswana and beyond.

GAYO Botswana is a youth-led environmental organization that focuses on building a generation of environmentally conscious leaders. Through its programs, the organization promotes climate justice, environmental education, community engagement, and sustainable development practices.

This call for applications is an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking practical experience, mentorship, and exposure in the environmental and climate change sector.

About GAYO Botswana

GAYO Botswana operates under the broader vision of the Green Africa Youth Organization network, which is dedicated to:

  • Promoting climate action and environmental sustainability
  • Supporting youth participation in environmental governance
  • Advancing zero waste and circular economy solutions
  • Building climate-resilient communities
  • Encouraging environmental education through eco-clubs and youth networks

The organization empowers young people to become active contributors to environmental solutions through volunteering, internships, advocacy, and grassroots engagement.

Available Opportunities

GAYO Botswana is currently recruiting for both volunteer and internship positions. All roles are designed to provide learning, professional development, and hands-on experience in environmental work.

  1. Volunteer Positions (Unpaid)

These roles are strictly voluntary and focus on building experience in environmental and organizational work.

  1. Zero Waste Volunteer (1 Position)

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting zero waste initiatives and awareness campaigns
  • Assisting in community education on waste reduction and recycling
  • Helping design and implement sustainability programs
  1. Administrative Volunteer (1 Position)

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting day-to-day organizational operations
  • Managing documentation and internal coordination tasks
  • Assisting with scheduling and communication activities
  1. Communications Volunteer (1 Position)

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting social media and digital communications
  • Creating awareness content on environmental issues
  • Assisting in storytelling and campaign visibility
  1. Eco-Clubs Volunteer (1 Position)

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting school and community eco-club programs
  • Assisting in environmental education activities
  • Engaging youth in sustainability projects and training sessions
  1. Internship Position

Youth Climate Council (YCC) Intern (1 Position)

This internship is a structured learning opportunity designed for individuals interested in climate policy, advocacy, and youth engagement.

Key focus areas include:

  • Climate change policy and governance exposure
  • Youth advocacy and leadership development
  • Stakeholder engagement and environmental networking
  • Participation in climate-related programs and discussions

This role is ideal for individuals who want to build a career in environmental policy, sustainability, or development work.

Important Notes

  • All volunteer positions are unpaid and intended for learning and professional development.
  • The Youth Climate Council (YCC) Internship is also a learning-focused opportunity, not a paid position.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted after application review.
  • Applicants are required to submit:
    • Updated CV (PDF format)
    • Cover Letter (PDF format)

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants are expected to meet the following criteria:

  • Strong interest in environmental sustainability and climate action
  • Willingness to learn and contribute to volunteer-based initiatives
  • Good communication and teamwork skills
  • Ability to commit time to the selected role
  • Passion for youth development and community engagement

Application Process

Applicants are required to submit their documents through the official application process (Google Form as provided by GAYO Botswana).

Required documents:

  • Updated CV (PDF)
  • Cover Letter (PDF)

Important confirmation:
Applicants must acknowledge that they understand the roles are unpaid and primarily focused on learning and professional development.

Contact email provided for inquiries:
konediakaridia499@gmail.com

Why This Opportunity Matters

This program offers valuable exposure for young people interested in environmental work. Participants will gain:

  • Practical experience in real environmental projects
  • Exposure to climate change and sustainability initiatives
  • Networking opportunities with youth and environmental professionals
  • Skills development in communication, administration, and advocacy
  • A foundation for future careers in environmental and development sectors

Deadline

The application deadline has not been specified in the available announcement. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to limited positions.

Conclusion

The GAYO Botswana Volunteer and Internship Programme 2026 is a powerful opportunity for young environmental enthusiasts to contribute to meaningful climate action while gaining essential professional experience. Whether through communications, administration, zero waste initiatives, eco-club engagement, or climate policy exposure, this program offers a platform for impactful youth involvement in environmental sustainability.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post GAYO Botswana Volunteer and Internship Opportunities 2026 – Call for Applications in Environmental Sustainability, Climate Action, and Youth Leadership Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MultiLAwa 2026 Fully Funded Marie Skłodowska-Curie Double Degree PhD Fellowships in Europe | 14 Open Positions for International Researchers
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The MultiLAwa Doctoral Network has officially opened applications for 14 fully funded Marie Skłodowska-Curie Double Degree PhD Fellowships under the Horizon Europe MSCA Doctoral Networks – Joint Doctorates programme. This prestigious European doctoral initiative is designed to train the next generation of multilingual and interdisciplinary researchers for the digital and Artificial Intelligence (AI) era. The […]

The post MultiLAwa 2026 Fully Funded Marie Skłodowska-Curie Double Degree PhD Fellowships in Europe | 14 Open Positions for International Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The MultiLAwa Doctoral Network has officially opened applications for 14 fully funded Marie Skłodowska-Curie Double Degree PhD Fellowships under the Horizon Europe MSCA Doctoral Networks – Joint Doctorates programme. This prestigious European doctoral initiative is designed to train the next generation of multilingual and interdisciplinary researchers for the digital and Artificial Intelligence (AI) era.

The programme, known as MultiLAwa (Multilingual Language Awareness in the European Digital Society), offers an exceptional opportunity for international students and early-career researchers who are passionate about linguistics, multilingualism, AI, digital communication, language technologies, sociolinguistics, and related fields.

Successful candidates will receive full funding, competitive salaries, international mobility opportunities, joint doctoral degrees from two European universities, and advanced interdisciplinary training across several countries in Europe.

The application deadline is 8 July 2026 at 11:59 pm (CEST).

About the MultiLAwa Doctoral Network

MultiLAwa is a Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Joint Doctorate programme funded under Horizon Europe. The initiative aims to prepare doctoral researchers for emerging professional demands linked to digital transformation, multilingual communication, and Generative AI technologies.

The programme focuses on developing experts in:

  • Language awareness
  • Multilingual communication
  • AI-driven language technologies
  • Computational linguistics
  • Digital linguistics
  • Sociolinguistics
  • Gender linguistics
  • Lexicography
  • Terminology
  • Language policy
  • Language teaching and didactics

The consortium also integrates expertise from other scientific and professional sectors, including:

  • Finance and Economic Sciences
  • Information Science and Engineering
  • Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Circular Economy

This broad interdisciplinary approach ensures that Doctoral Candidates gain the skills necessary to thrive in academic, governmental, technological, educational, and industrial environments.

What Makes the MultiLAwa PhD Fellowship Unique?

The MultiLAwa programme is not a traditional PhD programme. It is a highly international and interdisciplinary doctoral training network designed around multilingualism, mobility, and collaboration.

1. Double Degree PhD Structure

All selected Doctoral Candidates (DCs) will receive:

  • One joint doctoral degree (“cotutelle”)
  • Two PhD diplomas from two universities in different countries

Candidates will study and conduct research at multiple institutions across Europe.

2. Fully Funded Fellowship

All positions are fully funded for 36 months under the MSCA regulations.

The funding package includes:

  • Monthly living allowance: EUR 4010 (gross)
  • Monthly mobility allowance: EUR 710
  • Monthly family allowance: EUR 660 (if applicable)
  • Long-term leave allowance (if applicable)
  • Special needs allowance (if applicable)

Salary amounts will vary according to country correction coefficients.

3. International Research Mobility

Doctoral Candidates will:

  • Spend time at two host universities
  • Participate in international secondments
  • Conduct research stays ranging from a few days to six months
  • Collaborate with partner organisations and industries across Europe

4. Multilingual and Interdisciplinary Environment

The programme promotes plurilingualism and multicultural collaboration.

Internal communication and training activities will be conducted in both:

  • English
  • German

Researchers will also benefit from exposure to additional European languages and cultures.

5. Strong Career Development Support

Each Doctoral Candidate will receive:

  • A Personal Career Development Plan (PCDP)
  • Professional mentoring
  • Transferable skills training
  • Participation in conferences and workshops
  • Public engagement opportunities
  • Advanced research training

Research Areas Covered by MultiLAwa

The consortium combines expertise in several cutting-edge disciplines, including:

Linguistics and Language Studies

  • Applied Linguistics
  • Sociolinguistics
  • Gender Linguistics
  • Interactional Linguistics
  • Lexicography
  • Terminology
  • Language for Specific Purposes (LSP)
  • Didactics
  • Language Policy

Digital and AI Fields

  • Digital Linguistics
  • Computational Linguistics
  • Natural Language Processing (NLP)
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Language Technologies

Interdisciplinary Areas

  • Multimedia and Information Science
  • Finance and Economics
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Circular Economy

Host Countries and Salary Correction Coefficients

The fellowship includes placements across multiple European countries. Country correction coefficients apply to the living allowance.

2025 Country Correction Coefficients

  • Austria: 109.7%
  • Denmark: 132.8%
  • France: 118.1%
  • Germany: 101.2%
  • Italy: 95.3%
  • Poland: 74.1%
  • Portugal: 93.7%
  • Switzerland: 163.8%

Eligibility Criteria for the MultiLAwa Fellowship

To apply for the programme, applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements.

Applicants Must:

1. Not Hold a Doctoral Degree

Candidates must not already possess a PhD at the time of recruitment.

Even candidates who have defended their thesis but have not yet officially received their degree are not eligible.

2. Hold a Relevant Master’s Degree

Applicants must possess one of the following:

  • Master of Arts
  • Master of Translation
  • Master of Education
  • Master of Science (MSc)
  • Equivalent qualification

The degree must be in:

  • Linguistics
  • Translation Studies
  • Language Studies
  • Or a closely related discipline

Applicants must also demonstrate strong academic performance with distinction-level grades.

Minimum Academic Standards

Examples include:

  • UK: Above 70%
  • France: Above 14/20
  • Germany/Austria: Better than 2 (“gut”)
  • Switzerland: Above 5.5
  • Denmark: Above 7

3. Meet the MSCA Mobility Rule

Applicants must not have:

  • Lived
  • Worked
  • Studied

in the recruiting university’s country for more than 12 months during the last 36 months before recruitment.

4. Demonstrate Strong Language Skills

Applicants must have at least:

  • B2 level German
  • B2 level English

Language certificates or equivalent evidence must be provided.

Knowledge of additional languages is considered an advantage.

5. Be Interested in International and Interdisciplinary Research

Candidates should:

  • Enjoy multicultural collaboration
  • Be willing to travel internationally
  • Participate in research secondments
  • Work in interdisciplinary environments

What Training Will Fellows Receive?

Selected Doctoral Candidates will participate in a highly structured doctoral training programme.

Training opportunities include:

  • Research methodology workshops
  • AI and language technology training
  • Academic writing development
  • Public speaking and dissemination training
  • Transferable skills courses
  • International conferences
  • Collaborative projects
  • Public engagement activities

Online Information Session for Applicants

An online information session will be organized for interested applicants.

Information Session Details

  • Date: Tuesday 16 June 2026
  • Time: 2:00 pm (CEST)
  • Format: Online

The participation link will be shared later by the organizers.

Fellowship Duration and Start Date

Duration

  • 36 months full-time employment contract

Start Date

  • 1 November 2026

Why This Fellowship Matters in the AI and Digital Era

The growing influence of Artificial Intelligence, digital communication, multilingual societies, and language technologies has created increasing demand for experts who understand both human language and digital systems.

MultiLAwa responds directly to this demand by training researchers capable of:

  • Bridging linguistic and technological fields
  • Supporting multilingual digital societies
  • Developing ethical and inclusive AI communication systems
  • Advancing research in language technologies and cultural communication

The programme is particularly valuable for students interested in careers in:

  • Academia
  • AI and NLP industries
  • European institutions
  • Language policy
  • Translation technologies
  • International organisations
  • Education
  • Research institutes

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the MultiLAwa 2026 Marie Skłodowska-Curie Double Degree PhD Fellowships is:

8 July 2026 at 11:59 pm (CEST)

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early and attend the online information session.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official MultiLAwa recruitment portal.

Apply here

For more information on this PhD fellowship visit here 

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post MultiLAwa 2026 Fully Funded Marie Skłodowska-Curie Double Degree PhD Fellowships in Europe | 14 Open Positions for International Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ADB AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge 2026: Call for AI Experts, Data Scientists, and Transport Innovators Across Asia and the Pacific
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Road traffic injuries remain one of the leading causes of death globally, particularly in low- and middle-income countries where infrastructure, speed management systems, and urban planning often struggle to keep pace with rapid development and population growth. Across Asia and the Pacific, governments and development institutions are increasingly seeking data-driven approaches to improve road safety […]

The post ADB AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge 2026: Call for AI Experts, Data Scientists, and Transport Innovators Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Road traffic injuries remain one of the leading causes of death globally, particularly in low- and middle-income countries where infrastructure, speed management systems, and urban planning often struggle to keep pace with rapid development and population growth. Across Asia and the Pacific, governments and development institutions are increasingly seeking data-driven approaches to improve road safety and reduce preventable deaths.

To address this urgent challenge, Asian Development Bank has launched the AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge 2026, an international innovation competition designed to harness artificial intelligence, mobility data, and geospatial analysis to improve road safety systems across the region.

The initiative is being implemented in collaboration with the World Bank Development Impact Group, AI for Good, and International Telecommunication Union, with support from JFPR and HLTF.

The challenge invites data scientists, AI specialists, transport engineers, policy innovators, researchers, and multidisciplinary teams from ADB member countries to develop innovative AI-powered models capable of identifying where speed limits may not align with real-world road conditions and safety needs.

About the AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge

The AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge aims to explore how emerging technologies can transform the way governments and institutions understand and manage road safety risks.

By combining:

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • Large-scale mobility datasets
  • GPS probe data
  • Geospatial analysis
  • Machine learning tools
  • Road network information
  • Street-level imagery

the challenge seeks to uncover hidden risk patterns and generate practical solutions that can help policymakers make evidence-based decisions on road safety interventions.

Unlike traditional traffic monitoring systems that focus on whether drivers exceed speed limits, this challenge specifically asks participants to determine whether the posted speed limits themselves are appropriate for the actual road environment.

Core Challenge Question

Participants are expected to answer the following central question:

How might AI and mobility data be used to determine where speed limits are misaligned with real-world road conditions, thereby supporting evidence-based speed management across Asia and the Pacific?

This focus aligns with global Safe System principles, which emphasize designing transport systems that reduce the likelihood of fatal and serious injuries.

Objectives of the Competition

The competition aims to support the development of analytical tools and scalable methodologies that can:

  • Assess whether posted speed limits align with Safe System principles
  • Detect road segments where current speed limits may increase risk for vulnerable road users
  • Generate spatial outputs and geospatial visualizations
  • Help governments prioritize safety interventions
  • Support scalable, data-driven transport policy decisions
  • Improve evidence-based road safety management

Who Should Apply?

ADB is encouraging participation from experts and innovators with backgrounds in:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Data Science
  • Transport Engineering
  • Urban Planning
  • Geospatial Analysis
  • Public Policy
  • Machine Learning
  • Mobility Analytics
  • Road Safety Research

Teams can consist of:

  • Individual participants
  • Small multidisciplinary teams
  • Groups of up to five people

The competition encourages collaborative problem-solving that combines technical expertise with policy understanding.

Key Features Required in Submitted Solutions

Participants must design analytical models capable of performing several critical functions.

1. Safe Speed Assessment

Teams should develop methodologies that assess whether posted speed limits are appropriate based on:

  • Road function
  • Operating speeds
  • Land use patterns
  • Traffic intensity
  • Urban or rural classifications

The objective is to evaluate whether speed limits support safe mobility for all road users.

2. Risk Identification

Solutions should identify road segments where current speed limits may expose vulnerable users to elevated risks.

This includes areas affecting:

  • Pedestrians
  • Cyclists
  • Powered two-wheeler users
  • Communities near schools or markets

Participants are encouraged to use AI and data analysis techniques to uncover safety vulnerabilities not immediately visible through conventional assessments.

3. Policy-Ready Outputs

One of the most important aspects of the challenge is the production of practical outputs governments can use directly.

Solutions should therefore generate:

  • A Speed Safety Score
  • Geospatial visualizations
  • Interactive mapping outputs
  • Priority intervention recommendations

These outputs should support policymakers in identifying high-risk road segments for review or redesign.

Datasets Provided to Participants

Registered participants who agree to the Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) will receive access to anonymized and aggregated datasets.

Available Datasets Include:

GPS Probe Data

This dataset includes:

  • Operating speeds
  • 85th percentile speeds
  • Speed distributions
  • Posted speed limits
  • Traffic intensity indicators

Road Network Data

Participants will also receive road infrastructure data such as:

  • Functional road classifications
  • Urban/rural segmentation
  • Intersection density
  • Road segment lengths

Mapillary Street-Level Imagery

Street-level imagery datasets will provide:

  • Crowdsourced road images
  • Machine-learning identified road features
  • Road sign information

Optional Contextual Layers

Additional contextual datasets may include:

  • Population density
  • Land use patterns
  • School proximity indicators
  • Market proximity
  • Powered two-wheeler indicators

These datasets are intended to support comprehensive AI-driven analysis.

Timeline of the Competition

Application Phase

Deadline: 25 June 2026

Participants must submit:

  • AI analytical models
  • Geospatial analyses
  • Supporting documentation

before the deadline.

Review Phase

July 2026

Expert reviewers will evaluate all submissions.

The top five solutions will be shortlisted and officially announced in September 2026.

Refinement Phase

September 2026

Shortlisted teams will:

  • Build visualizations on ADB’s GIS platform
  • Refine their solutions
  • Incorporate technical feedback from reviewers and experts

Final Pitch Phase

October 2026

Finalists will present their solutions before a high-level jury panel.

This stage provides teams with the opportunity to showcase the practical impact and scalability of their innovation.

Why This Challenge Matters

Road crashes claim millions of lives globally every year, with vulnerable road users disproportionately affected.

Many existing road safety systems rely on outdated methods or incomplete data when determining appropriate speed limits. By integrating AI, mobility analytics, and geospatial intelligence, this initiative seeks to modernize road safety decision-making.

The challenge contributes to broader goals including:

  • Safer urban mobility
  • Sustainable transportation systems
  • Evidence-based policymaking
  • Reduced road fatalities
  • Improved infrastructure planning
  • Data-driven governance

It also demonstrates the growing role of AI in solving complex public-sector and development challenges.

Benefits of Participating

Participants in the AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge can gain several advantages:

  • International visibility
  • Exposure to global development institutions
  • Networking with leading AI and transport experts
  • Opportunity to contribute to road safety innovation
  • Potential integration into regional policy discussions
  • Experience working with large-scale mobility and geospatial datasets
  • Recognition from ADB and international partners

Shortlisted teams will also have the opportunity to refine and showcase their solutions on ADB’s GIS platform.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit solutions is:

25 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to register and begin working with the datasets as early as possible.

Official Website

For full details and registration, visit:
ADB Challenges Official Platform

Apply here  

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Competition opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post ADB AI for Safer Roads Innovation Challenge 2026: Call for AI Experts, Data Scientists, and Transport Innovators Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Adolescent Health Short Course 2026 at LSHTM: Apply for the International Training Program on Adolescent Health, Policy, and Development
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the 2026 Global Adolescent Health Short Course offered by the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM) in collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), UNICEF, Obafemi Awolowo University, the Institute of Child Health at the University of Ibadan, and the Public Health Foundation of India. This internationally recognized […]

The post Global Adolescent Health Short Course 2026 at LSHTM: Apply for the International Training Program on Adolescent Health, Policy, and Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the 2026 Global Adolescent Health Short Course offered by the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM) in collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), UNICEF, Obafemi Awolowo University, the Institute of Child Health at the University of Ibadan, and the Public Health Foundation of India.

This internationally recognized short course is designed to strengthen the capacity of professionals working in adolescent health and development by equipping them with evidence-based tools, conceptual frameworks, and policy approaches necessary to improve adolescent health systems worldwide.

The course will run from 7 September to 18 September 2026 and will be delivered in a hybrid format, allowing participants to join either face-to-face in London or online from anywhere in the world.

With growing concerns around adolescent mental health, sexual and reproductive health, violence, nutrition, substance use, infectious diseases, non-communicable diseases, and digital wellbeing, this course provides participants with advanced practical and policy-oriented training to address some of the most pressing public health challenges affecting adolescents globally.

Why Adolescent Health Matters

Investing in adolescent health is critical for achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), improving public health outcomes, and promoting sustainable economic development.

Adolescence is a transformative stage of life where behaviors, attitudes, and social patterns are established. Many adult diseases and health conditions — including HIV, cardiovascular diseases, lung cancer, mental health disorders, and substance abuse — often originate from behaviors developed during adolescence.

Despite increasing global evidence on effective interventions for adolescent health, many countries still face challenges related to limited resources, weak health systems, inadequate policies, and insufficient technical expertise. The Global Adolescent Health Course seeks to bridge these gaps by building the skills of policymakers, researchers, program implementers, and public health professionals.

About the Global Adolescent Health Course

The Global Adolescent Health course is jointly organized and taught by leading experts from:

  • London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM)
  • World Health Organization (WHO)
  • Obafemi Awolowo University, Nigeria
  • Institute of Child Health, University of Ibadan
  • Public Health Foundation of India
  • UNICEF

The course combines public health theory, evidence-based practice, and policy analysis to prepare professionals to design and implement effective adolescent health programs.

Course Dates

  • Course Period: 7 – 18 September 2026
  • Duration: 10 days
  • Learning Mode: Hybrid (Online and Face-to-Face)
  • Location for In-Person Participants: London, United Kingdom
  • Application Deadline: Friday, 7 August 2026 at 23:59 (UK Time)

Objectives of the Course

The course aims to equip participants with the knowledge and practical skills needed to improve adolescent health policies and interventions.

Participants will learn to:

  1. Understand the epidemiology of adolescent health issues
  2. Analyze evidence-based interventions for adolescent wellbeing
  3. Evaluate health systems and adolescent health programs
  4. Design and improve adolescent health policies and strategies
  5. Conduct research and evidence reviews related to adolescent health
  6. Understand behavioral theories, vulnerability, resilience, and risk frameworks
  7. Apply adolescent participation and youth engagement approaches
  8. Integrate adolescent health into national public health systems

Topics Covered in the Program

Participants will explore a wide range of adolescent health and development topics, including:

Core Themes

  • Adolescent development and wellbeing
  • Epidemiology of adolescent health
  • Risk and resilience theories
  • Youth engagement and participation
  • Intersectional perspectives in adolescent health
  • Adolescent rights and diversity
  • Health programming for adolescents
  • Digital-age adolescent wellbeing

Public Health Challenges

The course addresses major adolescent health challenges such as:

  • Sexual and reproductive health
  • Mental health
  • Nutrition
  • Drugs and alcohol
  • Violence and injuries
  • Infectious diseases
  • Non-communicable diseases

Policy and Research Areas

Participants will also learn about:

  • Situation analysis
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Health surveillance systems
  • Research methods for adolescent health
  • Policy development and implementation
  • International priorities and frameworks for adolescent health

Teaching Methods and Learning Experience

The course will be taught through:

  • Interactive lectures
  • Synchronous online sessions
  • Face-to-face learning
  • Pre-recorded case studies
  • Group projects
  • Participatory discussions
  • Evidence review exercises

Participants will receive electronic access to:

  • PowerPoint presentations
  • Course materials
  • Key academic references
  • Supporting resources

The course faculty includes internationally recognized experts in adolescent health programming, research, and policy development.

Who Should Apply?

The course is specifically designed for mid-career professionals involved in adolescent health and development.

Suitable applicants include:

  • Public health professionals
  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Program implementers
  • NGO professionals
  • Development practitioners
  • Health sector managers
  • International organization staff

Applicants should have:

  • Experience in adolescent health or related fields
  • Strong interest in public health and development
  • Good command of English

The course is public health-oriented rather than clinically focused.

Intended Learning Outcomes

By the end of the course, participants will be able to:

  • Critically evaluate adolescent health indicators and burden measures
  • Apply research evidence to policy and programming
  • Assess health system capacities for adolescent interventions
  • Analyze policy effectiveness at different governance levels
  • Strengthen adolescent health strategies in their countries and organizations

Testimonials from Previous Participants

Past participants have described the course as transformative and highly practical.

Professionals from organizations such as UNICEF, UNFPA, UNESCO, ministries of health, and international NGOs praised the course for:

  • Its participatory teaching style
  • Practical policy relevance
  • Strong evidence-based approach
  • Real-world applications
  • Networking opportunities
  • Global perspectives on adolescent wellbeing

Course Fees for 2026

Standard Fee

  • £1,932

Discounted Fee

  • £966 for nationals of Low- and Middle-Income Countries (LMICs) currently residing in LMICs

Applicants requesting the discounted fee must provide:

  • Passport copy
  • Proof of current residence
  • Evidence of LMIC status

Please note that no scholarships or fee waivers are available for the 2026 intake.

Important Information for Applicants

Application Deadline

Applications close on:

Friday, 7 August 2026 at 23:59 (UK Time)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early because the course may close before the official deadline if spaces become full.

Visa Information

Participants attending in person are responsible for obtaining the appropriate UK visa.

LSHTM can provide supporting documentation if required.

Accommodation

LSHTM provides accommodation guidance and hotel recommendations for participants attending physically in London.

Additional Notes

  • Participants must bring their own laptops
  • Passport identification is mandatory for registration
  • Participants requiring visas should begin the process early
  • LSHTM may cancel the course if enrollment numbers are insufficient

Apply Here 

Why You Should Apply

This course offers a rare opportunity to gain advanced global expertise in adolescent health from some of the world’s leading institutions in public health and development.

Participants will benefit from:

  • International networking opportunities
  • Practical policy and programming skills
  • Exposure to global adolescent health experts
  • Evidence-based learning approaches
  • Hybrid learning flexibility
  • Real-world case studies and research

Professionals working in public health, youth development, education, international development, and health policy will find this course highly valuable for career growth and program impact.

For professionals seeking to strengthen adolescent health systems and improve outcomes for young people globally, this course provides an exceptional learning opportunity.

For more information on this short course, click here 

Discover more short course opportunities on OFY here 

The post Global Adolescent Health Short Course 2026 at LSHTM: Apply for the International Training Program on Adolescent Health, Policy, and Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Interledger Fellowship Program 2026 | $92,000 Funding for Digital Financial Inclusion Projects
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the 2026 Interledger Fellowship Program, a prestigious global fellowship supporting innovators, researchers, developers, educators, advocates, and creators working to remove barriers to digital financial inclusion. The Interledger Foundation Fellowship is a fully funded 12-month program designed to support individuals building solutions that help underserved communities access and participate meaningfully […]

The post Interledger Fellowship Program 2026 | $92,000 Funding for Digital Financial Inclusion Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the 2026 Interledger Fellowship Program, a prestigious global fellowship supporting innovators, researchers, developers, educators, advocates, and creators working to remove barriers to digital financial inclusion.

The Interledger Foundation Fellowship is a fully funded 12-month program designed to support individuals building solutions that help underserved communities access and participate meaningfully in digital financial systems.

Selected fellows will receive a total compensation package of up to $92,000, including direct fellowship funding and project support.

Applications will remain open until June 15, 2026, at 11:59 PM EDT.

About the Interledger Fellowship Program

The Interledger Fellowship Program is a global initiative focused on advancing equitable access to digital financial systems and economic participation.

The fellowship supports individuals whose work addresses real-world financial exclusion challenges, including:

  • High transaction costs
  • Documentation barriers
  • Limited financial literacy
  • Exclusionary regulations
  • Inaccessible digital infrastructure
  • Lack of community-centered financial tools

The program seeks to empower fellows who are developing innovative approaches that make financial systems more inclusive, accessible, and community-driven.

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship runs for:

  • 12 months

During this period, fellows will work on their proposed projects while engaging with the Interledger Foundation’s global ecosystem.

Funding and Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

Fellowship Compensation

  • $72,000 total fellowship compensation

Project Support Funding

  • Additional $20,000 stipend for:
    • Research
    • Development
    • Organizing
    • Advocacy
    • Community engagement
    • Technical implementation

Total Funding

  • Up to $92,000 overall support

This funding is intended to allow fellows to focus fully on impactful projects that advance financial inclusion and digital participation.

Focus Areas for 2026

The Interledger Foundation is seeking proposals in several critical areas related to digital financial inclusion.

1. Research

Projects that study:

  • Regulatory barriers
  • Economic challenges
  • Technical obstacles
  • Social exclusion in digital finance systems

The fellowship welcomes evidence-based research exploring why certain populations remain excluded from digital financial services.

2. Education and Capacity Building

Projects that:

  • Create educational resources
  • Develop financial literacy programs
  • Build community training initiatives
  • Increase awareness of digital financial tools

This area is especially focused on communities with limited exposure to digital financial technologies.

3. Policy and Standards

Projects advocating for:

  • Inclusive regulatory frameworks
  • Open financial systems
  • Technical interoperability standards
  • Consumer protection without exclusion

Applicants working on governance, policy reform, and open standards are encouraged to apply.

4. Solutions for Underserved Markets

The fellowship supports innovators creating:

  • Financial tools
  • Digital payment systems
  • Open-source technologies
  • Accessible infrastructure
  • Community-centered financial products

Projects should focus on populations currently underserved by existing systems.

5. Ecosystem Strengthening

Projects that build:

  • Communities
  • Networks
  • Support systems
  • Collaborative platforms

The goal is to help people navigate and benefit from digital financial services more effectively.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship welcomes diverse applicants from across sectors and disciplines.

Ideal applicants include:

  • Researchers
  • Software developers
  • Open-source contributors
  • Educators
  • Community organizers
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Policy experts
  • Financial inclusion advocates
  • Artists and storytellers
  • Human rights advocates
  • Digital rights activists

The program especially values applicants working closely with underserved communities.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Digital financial systems increasingly shape how people:

  • Access opportunities
  • Receive payments
  • Build businesses
  • Participate in society

Yet millions remain excluded due to systemic barriers.

The Interledger Fellowship aims to support people building more equitable systems where access to financial tools is:

  • Affordable
  • Inclusive
  • Community-centered
  • Privacy-conscious
  • Accessible to all

The program recognizes that financial inclusion is not only about technology, but also about dignity, participation, and economic justice.

Inspiration from Previous Fellows

The fellowship builds on the success of the Interledger Ambassadorship Program, which since 2020 has supported global innovators working in:

  • Open-source technology
  • Accessibility
  • Advocacy
  • Education
  • Research
  • Community engagement

Past fellows have explored themes including:

  • Digital equity
  • Financial rights
  • Data governance
  • Accessibility in technology
  • Community-centered finance
  • Diaspora financial systems
  • Inclusive digital infrastructure

Application Timeline

Applications Open

  • April 1, 2026

Application Deadline

  • June 15, 2026
  • 11:59 PM EDT

Late applications may not be accepted.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the official Submittable platform.

Applicants should review:

  • The FAQ
  • The official Application Guide
  • Eligibility requirements
  • Proposal instructions

before applying.

APPLY HERE

Why You Should Apply

The Interledger Fellowship is one of the most impactful global opportunities for innovators working at the intersection of:

  • Technology
  • Finance
  • Community development
  • Human rights
  • Open systems
  • Economic inclusion

The fellowship provides:

  • Significant funding support
  • Global visibility
  • Access to an international network
  • Space to focus on impactful work
  • Opportunities to shape the future of digital finance

For researchers, developers, educators, and advocates committed to equitable economic participation, this fellowship offers both resources and a powerful platform.

For more information on this fellowship visit here 

For more Fellowship Opportunities, visit OFY here.

The post Interledger Fellowship Program 2026 | $92,000 Funding for Digital Financial Inclusion Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050. Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures, this […]

The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050.

Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures, this international initiative encourages people everywhere to use the power of imagination and storytelling to envision solutions to today’s social and environmental challenges.

The competition is open globally with no age restrictions and no geographical limitations, making it one of the most inclusive international creative writing opportunities currently available.

Participants stand a chance to win a grand prize of USD 10,000 while contributing to conversations about the future of humanity, sustainability, innovation, and social transformation.

What is the Social Fiction Design Competition?

The Social Fiction Design Competition is an international writing competition focused on “Social Fiction” — a form of imaginative storytelling similar to science fiction, but centered on social innovation and human progress rather than futuristic technology alone.

The competition invites participants to imagine:

  • What the world could look like in 2050
  • How young people may live in the future
  • How societies can overcome inequality and injustice
  • What sustainable communities might become
  • How governments, businesses, and communities can work together for positive change

The goal is to inspire new ways of thinking about social innovation and encourage people to imagine the world they truly want to build.

According to Nobel Peace Laureate Muhammad Yunus:

“If we do not imagine, it will not be done. If we imagine, someday it will happen.”

This powerful idea forms the foundation of the competition.

What is Social Fiction?

Social Fiction is a storytelling approach that imagines a future society shaped by positive social transformation.

Unlike traditional science fiction, which often focuses heavily on futuristic machines or scientific advancement, Social Fiction focuses on:

  • Human-centered innovation
  • Social justice
  • Sustainability
  • Equality
  • Community transformation
  • Ethical development
  • Inclusive economies
  • Environmental healing

The concept encourages writers to creatively explore solutions to real-world problems and imagine societies where communities thrive.

The organizers believe that imagination can inspire real-world innovation and policy change.

Competition Theme: The Life of a Young Person in 2050

Participants are asked to imagine and describe:

“How do you wish to see the life of a young person in the year 2050?”

Writers are encouraged to take readers on a journey into a future world that is free from the social and environmental injustices affecting communities today.

The competition emphasizes optimism, innovation, and bold thinking.

Applicants should imagine a future where systems, institutions, and communities have evolved to create better opportunities and improved quality of life for younger generations.

Writing Requirements

Participants must submit an original Social Fiction piece between:

  • Minimum: 1,000 words
  • Maximum: 5,000 words

The writing should creatively explore a fictional world in 2050 while addressing one or more major social or environmental issues.

Suggested Focus Areas

Participants may choose to focus on one or several of the following areas:

  • Climate Change
  • Agriculture
  • Technology and Innovation
  • Micro-credit
  • Transport
  • Literature
  • Music
